title: files-panel
content:
Files Panel

The Files panel manages binary file attachments embedded directly in the GLB file. Attachments travel with the model when it is saved and exported, so collaborators receive all supporting files in a single .glb.

Supported attachment types include images (PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, SVG…), PDFs, office documents, archives, and any other binary file.

Panel Buttons

  • + Add files… – Opens a file picker that allows multiple files to be selected at once. If a file with the same name already exists, you are prompted to replace it.

  • 📋 Paste image… – Reads an image from the system clipboard and adds it as an attachment. Useful for quick screenshots.

  • 🖼 New image… – Creates a new blank canvas image that can be drawn on with the built-in image editor.

  • ⬇ Download all as ZIP – Packages all attachments into a single ZIP archive and downloads it. Shown only when at least one attachment is present.

  • ↗ Open all viewable… – Opens every browser-viewable attachment (images, PDFs, text, video, audio) in separate floating preview windows. After the windows appear, you are offered the option to automatically arrange them as a tile. Shown only when at least one viewable attachment is present.

  • ✏ Edit all images… – Opens the built-in image editor for every image attachment simultaneously. After the windows appear, you are offered the option to automatically arrange them as a tile. Shown only when at least one image attachment is present.

Per-Attachment Folder

Each attachment is listed as a collapsible folder named filename (size). If the attachment has a comment, a 💬 prefix is shown. The folder contains the following actions:

  • ↗ Open – Opens the file in a floating preview window inside the app. Multiple files can be open at the same time, each in its own window. Available for any file type the browser can display directly: raster images (PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF, SVG), PDFs, plain text files, video, and audio. For other types (ZIP, DOCX, XLSX, etc.) use ⬇ Download instead and open locally. Clicking ↗ Open on a file that is already open focuses the existing window.

  • ✏ Edit – Opens the built-in image editor for this attachment (available for image files only — PNG, JPG, WebP, GIF). See the Image Editor section below for a full description of available tools.

  • ✏ Edit PDF… – Opens selected PDF pages in the image editor, then lets you export the changes back into the PDF. Edited pages are stored as images inside the PDF; other pages stay unchanged. Available for PDF attachments only.

  • 📄 Manage PDF pages… – Opens a page manager to reorder, delete, or insert pages (blank page, image file, image from Files, or pages from another PDF). Save overwrites the original or creates a new PDF attachment. Available for PDF attachments only.

  • 🖼 Convert to images… – Renders each PDF page to a separate PNG or JPG attachment. Available for PDF attachments only.

  • ⬇ Download – Downloads the file to your local disk.

  • ✕ Delete – Removes the attachment from the scene permanently.

File Preview Windows

Viewable attachments open as floating, draggable, resizable windows. Multiple files can be previewed simultaneously, each in its own window. All preview windows share a single toolbar at the top of the screen.

Shared Toolbar

  • ⊞ Tile – Automatically arranges all open preview windows into a non-overlapping grid.

  • ✕ Close all – Closes every open preview window.

Per-Window Controls (title bar)

  • Filename – Click to rename the attachment (extension is read-only).

  • 🔤 OCR – Recognize text in an image (images only).

  • ✏ Edit / ✏ Edit PDF – Open the image editor or PDF page editor (images and PDFs only).

  • 📄 Pages – Open the PDF page manager (PDFs only).

  • 🖼 → Images – Convert PDF pages to PNG/JPG attachments (PDFs only).

  • 💬 Comment – Toggle a rich-text comment panel at the bottom of the window.

  • ⤢ Maximize – Maximizes the window to fill the screen below the shared toolbar. Double-clicking the title bar has the same effect.

  • ✕ Close – Closes this preview window. Press Esc to close the active window.

Preview by file type

  • Images – Pan (drag), zoom (scroll wheel), double-click to fit. Pinch-to-zoom on touch devices.

  • PDF, text, HTML – Rendered in the browser's built-in viewer inside the window.

  • Video / audio – Native HTML5 player with controls.

Image Editor

The built-in canvas-based image editor opens as a floating, draggable window. Multiple images can be edited simultaneously, each in its own window. All windows share a single toolbar at the top of the screen.

Toolbar — View & Navigation

  • ✋ Pan – Drag to pan around the image. Hold Ctrl in any other tool to temporarily pan. Scroll wheel to zoom. Press F to fit the image to the window.

  • ⛶ Crop – Drag a rectangle to define the crop region, then click ✔ Apply to crop or ✕ Cancel to discard.

  • ⬚ Select – Drag a rectangle to select an area. Drag inside the selection to move it. Press Delete or Backspace to erase the selected area. Press Esc to deselect.

Toolbar — Drawing Tools

  • ✎ Pen – Freehand drawing. Stroke width is set by the Size control.

  • 🖌 Highlight – Semi-transparent freehand brush, useful for highlighting areas without fully obscuring the image.

  • 🩹 Eraser – Erases pixels to transparency (or to the background color if BG is enabled). Toggle between circle and square eraser shape with the adjacent ○ / □ button.

  • T Text – Click anywhere on the image to open a text input dialog. Type the text and confirm to place it. Font, font size, and color are controlled by the toolbar options.

Toolbar — Shape Tools

  • ▭ Rectangle – Drag to draw a rectangle. Toggle filled vs. outlined with the ◻ / ■ fill button.

  • ⬭ Ellipse – Drag to draw an ellipse. Supports the same fill toggle.

  • ╱ Line – Drag to draw a straight line.

  • → Arrow – Drag to draw an arrow from the start point to the tip.

  • ① Callout – Click to place a numbered circle marker. The counter auto-increments with each placement. Use ↺① to reset the counter to 1.

  • ⊞ Blur – Drag a rectangle to pixelate/blur that area. The Size value controls the pixel block size.

Toolbar — Options

  • Color – Drawing and text color picker.

  • BG – Optional background color for erasure, callout fill, and text background. Enable with the checkbox; leave unchecked for transparent.

  • Size – Stroke / brush width (1–100 px).

  • Font / Font size – Font family and size in px for the Text tool.

  • ⊞ Tile – Automatically arranges all open editor windows into a non-overlapping grid.

Per-Window Controls (title bar)

  • ↩ Undo / ↪ Redo – Step backward or forward through the edit history (up to 30 steps).

  • ⏮ Show original – Jumps back to the original unedited image without clearing the undo stack.

  • ↻ Rotate 90° CW / ↺ Rotate 90° CCW – Rotates the canvas 90 degrees clockwise or counter-clockwise.

  • ⇄ Flip Horizontal / ⇅ Flip Vertical – Mirrors the canvas.

  • ⊡ Resize – Opens a dialog to scale the image to a new pixel size.

  • ▦ Canvas resize – Opens a dialog to change the canvas dimensions without scaling the existing image (adds or crops borders).

  • 💾 Save (overwrite) – Saves the edited image back to the Files panel, replacing the original attachment.

  • 💾✎ Save as new – Saves the edited image as a new separate attachment, leaving the original unchanged.

  • ⬇ Download – Downloads the current canvas to disk without saving to the attachment store.

  • ⤢ Maximize – Maximizes or restores the editor window. Double-clicking the title bar has the same effect.

  • ✕ Close – Closes this editor window. Unsaved changes are discarded.

Zoom & Status bar

The status bar at the bottom of each window shows the current zoom level (click the field to type a percentage; click the label to reset to 100%). The cursor position in image pixels is shown on the right.

PDF Editing

PDF attachments can be edited without leaving the app:

  1. Click ✏ Edit PDF… in the attachment folder or ✏ Edit PDF in the file preview window title bar.

  2. Choose which pages to edit (all, a single page, or ranges such as 1, 3-5).

  3. Choose render quality (PNG/JPG, scale or DPI) — same options as Convert to images.

  4. Edit each page in the image editor (draw, text, crop, etc.).

  5. After saving a page, confirm export back to PDF — overwrite the original or save as a new PDF file.

Important: edited pages are rasterized. Text on those pages can no longer be copied or searched. Unedited pages keep their original vector content.

PDF Page Management

Structural changes to PDF attachments (page order, deletion, insertion) are separate from content editing:

  1. Click 📄 Manage PDF pages… in the attachment folder or 📄 Pages in the file preview window title bar.

  2. Drag page thumbnails to reorder, or use the ↑ / ↓ buttons on each card.

  3. Click ✕ on a card to delete a page (at least one page must remain).

  4. Click a page card to select where new pages will be inserted (after the selected page).

  5. Use + Blank page, + Image file, + From Files, or + From PDF to add pages.

  6. Click Save — overwrite the original PDF or save as a new file (name-pages-edited.pdf).

Pages added from another PDF keep their vector content. Image pages are scaled to fit the reference page size. Use ✏ Edit PDF… separately if you need to draw or annotate page content.

Using Attachments in Documents

Images stored in the Files panel can be inserted directly into any Docs panel document without re-uploading. In the document editor toolbar, use 📎🖼 Insert image from Files to open a thumbnail picker and select the image.

Storage Notes

  • Attachments are stored as Base64-encoded data inside the GLB file's userData. Large attachments significantly increase the GLB file size.

  • The Files panel is cleared when Edit → Clear scene. Keep docs + files is not used — a plain scene clear removes attachments. Use that action if you want to reload a model while keeping your files.
